author: w3Lb
content: Plan (revalidated post-#109: justification is now MEMORY — sed edits 10GB files, ripsed holds input+output in RAM; speed parity already achieved): in process_one_file, files >= 8 MiB take a streaming path when no undo entry will be recorded (--no-undo, or size > undo.max_file_bytes cap > 0), op is not multiline, and the file has no BOM (UTF-16/BOM files need transcode -> buffered). Streams BufReader -> LineProcessor -> BufWriter(NamedTempFile), per-line terminator passthrough; temp persisted only if changes > 0 (backup taken just before persist so it captures the original); invalid UTF-8 mid-stream aborts cleanly leaving the original untouched. FileOutcome gains notes (stderr lines that aren't errors — the undo-skip notice from workers) and total_line_changes (streaming collects only the first 50 Changes for display; the count is exact). print_file_diff takes the true total for its '... and N more' line. Behavior note for CHANGELOG: streamed files keep each line's own terminator (no majority-vote normalization) — only observable on mixed-ending >=8MiB no-undo files. JSON mode stays buffered (content-cache semantics).
